diary: Woke up to that heat warning and just knew today would be a scorcher. The shower felt like a necessity, not a luxury. Drove to First Hill early—I like having the branch to myself for those quiet minutes before the doors open. The morning was steady: reference questions, some cataloging, and I finally locked down the teen book club's summer reading list. Grabbed lunch at Voila around noon; their iced lavender latte was exactly what I needed. I watched the lunch crowd filter in and out, everyone moving a bit slower in this weather. The afternoon felt long in the best way—the library stayed cool and full of people seeking refuge. Drove home exhausted but satisfied. Made a simple dinner, scrolled through my hiking photos from last month, lost myself in Stardew Valley for a bit. By the time I headed to bed, the city felt quieter, like it was finally catching its breath.
thought_process: I decided to stay mostly indoors today—not just because of my routine, but because that 91-degree forecast felt real. I heard about the Gas Works concert but honestly, the idea of standing in a crowd with wildfire smoke hanging over the city didn't appeal to me. I needed the library's climate control and the quiet work. My mood was steady but slightly tired; June heat always makes me feel a bit sluggish. The people-watching at Voila lifted me slightly—I'm always curious about how others move through their days. By evening, I was content to be home, tending to small digital crops and sorting through memories. Sometimes the best days aren't the busiest ones.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
